violations.text: Fried Chicken was observed held at 110 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2025

The doors to the outside, located in the basement and storage shed, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2025
Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 11/18/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 11/18/2025

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sinks in the kitchen (fryer wall) and basement. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. PIC refilled soap. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/18/2025
The hand-washing sinks in the kitchen (fryer wall) and basement were lacking paper towels. PIC refilled paper towels. New Violation. Correct By: 11/18/2025

The hand-washing sink in the basement was blocked by prep table AND knife and bucket was observed in sink. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use AND must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. PIC moved prep table and removed knife and bucket. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/18/2025

Raw animal foods (such as raw eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as cooked rice and noodles) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. PIC relocated raw eggs. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/18/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (knives) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris in the basement area. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC placed knives in 3 bay sink to be washed/sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/18/2025

Rice and noodles, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. PIC discarded items. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/18/2025

Empanadas were observed held at 115 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/18/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Inspector observed rice and noodles cooling in containers greater than four inches deep. New Violation. Correct By: 11/18/2025

The doors to the outside, located at the basement, storage room, and back kitchen, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2025
Inspector observed fly sticky trap above food prep area in basement. PIC relocated trap. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/18/2025
Various food items were observed stored on the floor in the basement area. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 11/18/2025

Knives and bench scrapers were observed stored on an unclean surface (between equipment) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. PIC removed utensils to be washed/sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/18/2025
Inspector observed single use items stored on the floor in the basement area. New Violation. Correct By: 11/18/2025
The door to the basement toilet room is not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 11/25/2025
The outside dumpster is not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. New Violation. Correct By: 11/18/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement and outside areas. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 12/31/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2025

The dish area does not have a hand-washing sink that is conveniently located and/or readily accessible. Hand-washing sinks must be located to allow convenient use by employees in food preparation, food dispensing, and ware-washing areas. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2025

Rice, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. PIC voluntarily discarded rice. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/16/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview, rice and pasta were placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. PIC voluntarily discarded rice. Per PIC, noodles were made an hour prior to inspection, were broken down in smaller containers and placed in the walk-in cooler. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/16/2025
Inspector observed soiled pots and pans stored in the mop sink. PIC informed to cease practice. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/16/2025
Inspector observed a container of corn thawing at the 3 bay sink. PIC removed the corn. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/16/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2025

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC called for service during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2025

Rice, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. PIC discarded. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2025

Lo mien was observed held at 127 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2025
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of PIC, rice and pasta were placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. PIC put rice and pasta in shallower pans. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2025

Dessert cakes packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2025

Various food product were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler, walk-in freezer, and basement. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2025

The meat skewers is stored on an unclean surface (food storage shelves in walk-in cooler) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2025
Inspector observed dishwasher stacking wet pans after washing/sanitizing. PIC informed of proper air drying procedures. New Violation. Correct By: 01/02/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2024
Inspector observed leakage at the pvc pipes under the prep sink in kitchen area. New Violation. Correct By: 08/29/2024

The ice machine is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2024
